channel.title: The Grant Desk - Visual & Media Arts, EU
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Visual & Media Arts, EU.

item.title: ZAMEK Artist Residency 2027 (ZAMEK Culture Centre, Poznan)
item.description:
Organisation: ZAMEK Culture Centre (Centrum Kultury ZAMEK)

Location: Poznan, Poland

Award: Accommodation in an apartment in the former imperial residence, studio access, a fee of PLN 6,000 gross per month (contract for specific work), a production budget of PLN 5,000 to 15,000 gross depending on length of stay and project, plus curatorial support and publicity. No application fee listed.

Duration: 4 to 8 weeks during 2027 (Conceptual or Operational Residency format)

Deadline: 27 Jul 2026

Open call for ZAMEK Culture Centre's 2027 Artist-in-Residence programme in Poznan. Supports artists working across visual arts, performance, choreography, dance, music, sound art, architecture and filmmaking (literature is excluded). Two formats: a Conceptual Residency (creative stay, no requirement to complete or present a final work, with public meetings at least every two weeks) and an Operational Residency (concluding with an artistic event such as an exhibition, concert or screening, plus regular public meetings). Selected artists receive accommodation in the former imperial residence, a studio, a fee of PLN 6,000 gross per month, a production budget of PLN 5,000 to 15,000 gross, curatorial support and publicity. Residencies run 4 to 8 weeks in 2027. ZAMEK is particularly interested in projects that engage changing realities, the current moment and the future (climate and migration crises, the war in Ukraine, social and political issues) or the context, history and architecture of the Castle; project scale must fit the programme budget, and projects should not rely on external co-financing. Download the Terms & Conditions on the ZAMEK site to apply. Deadline: 27 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: APOSTAXIS Artist Residency & Exhibition 2026 (Ukraine / Cyprus)
item.description:
Organisation: ZMINA (Ukraine) with Cypriot partner organisation

Location: Ternopil, Ukraine and Larnaca, Cyprus (hybrid participation possible)

Award: Fully funded: artist fee of EUR 1,800 (including tax; paid in equivalent UAH for Ukrainian artists), EUR 300 production budget, plus travel, accommodation, meals and curatorial support. No application fee.

Duration: 20-day residency, 6 to 28 October 2026, with a group exhibition in Larnaca in January 2027

Deadline: 26 Jul 2026

APOSTAXIS is an international residency and exhibition programme exploring displacement, memory, identity and cultural resilience. It selects four visual artists (three from Ukraine and one from Cyprus) for two simultaneous 20-day residencies in Ternopil, Ukraine and Larnaca, Cyprus (6 to 28 October 2026), followed by post-production, a digital exhibition, and a curated physical exhibition in Larnaca in January 2027. ELIGIBILITY: visual artists from Ukraine and Cyprus with lived experience or family heritage connected to displacement, working in community-engaged or research-based practices (installation, mixed media, participatory, social practice, documentary, performance, archive-based work). Artists unable to travel due to war-related restrictions or personal circumstances remain eligible via hybrid participation. Fully funded: EUR 1,800 artist fee (equivalent UAH for Ukrainian artists), EUR 300 production budget, travel, accommodation, meals and curatorial support; no application fee. Deadline: 26 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Fonds voor Cultuurparticipatie: Open Schemes for Cultural Institutions 2025-2028 (rolling, Netherlands)
item.description:
Organisation: Fonds voor Cultuurparticipatie

Location: Netherlands and the Caribbean parts of the Kingdom. Cultural institutions, vocational education institutions and interdisciplinary makers.

Award: EUR 5,000 to EUR 75,000 depending on the scheme (e.g. cultural education in mbo EUR 5,000-55,000, in vmbo/vso/pro EUR 10,000-65,000, design-based learning platforms up to EUR 75,000, interdisciplinary makers EUR 12,500-50,000). No application fee.

Duration: Schemes run within the 2025-2028 policy period; multiple schemes currently open.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The Fonds voor Cultuurparticipatie, one of the Dutch national cultural funds (annual budget around EUR 30 million), currently has several schemes open for applications: cultural education in vocational education (mbo, EUR 5,000-55,000), cultural education in vmbo/vso/pro (EUR 10,000-65,000), cultural education for the Caribbean parts of the Kingdom (EUR 5,000-50,000), an open call for design-based learning platforms (up to EUR 75,000) and an interdisciplinary scheme for professional makers (EUR 12,500-50,000). Most schemes target cultural institutions, in some cases in partnership with schools. See the subsidies overview for per-scheme conditions. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: VSBfonds: Kunst & Cultuur Donations (rolling, Netherlands)
item.description:
Organisation: VSBfonds

Location: European Netherlands. Private legal entities (foundations, associations); applicants without a registered legal form are also eligible.

Award: No fixed minimum or maximum; the requested amount must be in proportion to other funding sources. No application fee.

Duration: Project funding; apply at least four months before the start date (two months for amateur arts initiatives).

Deadline: Rolling / undated

VSBfonds supports cultural initiatives that make it possible for everyone in the Netherlands to take part in art and culture, spanning ten programme lines from professional performances to community arts. Applications are accepted year-round with no deadlines from foundations, associations and applicants without a formal legal form, and must be submitted at least four months before the initiative starts (two months for amateur arts). A two-minute online quickscan checks eligibility before the formal application. No fixed minimum or maximum amount; the request should be proportionate to other funding. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Mondriaan Fund: Delfina Foundation Residency 2027 (London)
item.description:
Organisation: Mondriaan Fund / Delfina Foundation

Location: London, UK. For visual artists and curators based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom, funded by the Mondriaan Fund.

Award: Subsidy of EUR 9,188 for an artist or EUR 7,232 for a curator/art observer toward travel, material and living costs (a 10 percent personal contribution is automatically deducted by the Mondriaan Fund); studio, living space and Delfina Foundation support are paid directly by the Mondriaan Fund. Contribution for Caribbean-based artists can be increased for higher travel costs. No application fee.

Duration: Three months: 27 September to 17 December 2027

Deadline: 27 Aug 2026

Mondriaan Fund-supported working period at London's Delfina Foundation, one of the UK's best known artist-in-residence programmes, housed at 29-31 Catherine Place near Buckingham Palace with space for 6 to 8 international residents (own room, shared kitchen and facilities). Delfina promotes artistic exchange and experiment, offering visual artists and curators a platform to carry out research, develop ideas and position their practice within the global discourse, with introductions to the London art scene and Delfina's international network. The programme includes visits to museums, galleries and non-profit platforms, discussions with London-based artists, portfolio reviews with curators and regular networking lunches; residents are expected to take part. Applicants are not asked for a concrete project proposal but must submit a clear motivation: why they wish to take part, what research they want to conduct, and how it can contribute to Delfina's ongoing programme. WHO: visual artists and curators based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom; duo applications not possible; application must be in English. One work period available: 27 September to 17 December 2027. Selection by an advisory committee with a Delfina representative; processing may exceed three months. DEADLINE: Thursday 27 August 2026, before 16:00 Dutch time / 11:00 Caribbean time.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Konrad and Pawel Jarodzki Artist-in-Residence Programme 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Krzyzowa Foundation for Mutual Understanding in Europe

Location: Krzyzowa, Poland. For residents of Creative Europe countries other than Poland (Netherlands eligible).

Award: Accommodation, financial support, and a production budget (amounts not published). 6-week residency. No application fee.

Duration: Residency 21 September to 31 October 2026.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Krzyzowa Foundation's Konrad and Pawel Jarodzki Artist-in-Residence Programme hosts an artist for six weeks (21 September to 31 October 2026) at the historic Krzyzowa estate in Lower Silesia, providing accommodation, financial support, and a production budget (amounts not published on the call page). ELIGIBILITY: residents of Creative Europe countries other than Poland. Deadline 31 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Fonds 21: Kunst & Cultuur Project Grants (rolling, Netherlands)
item.description:
Organisation: Fonds 21

Location: Netherlands. Dutch foundations, associations and non-profit BVs/NVs; projects must reach a broad and/or new audience in the Netherlands.

Award: Minimum request EUR 10,000, no stated maximum; around EUR 11.5 million awarded per year across all pillars. No application fee.

Duration: Project funding; apply at least four months before the project starts.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Fonds 21 supports professional, high-quality art and culture projects by Dutch non-profit organizations (foundations, associations and non-profit BVs/NVs), across performing arts, visual arts, film, literature and interdisciplinary work, with additional pillars for arts education and youth & society. The minimum request is EUR 10,000 with no stated maximum, and roughly EUR 11.5 million is available annually. Applications are accepted year-round via the online portal (after a short eligibility snelcheck) and must be submitted at least four months before the project start date. Free-of-charge events are generally not supported; the fund expects most programming to be paid-access. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Het Cultuurfonds: Project Grants for Foundations and Associations (rolling, Netherlands)
item.description:
Organisation: Het Cultuurfonds (formerly Prins Bernhard Cultuurfonds)

Location: Netherlands. National office plus provincial chapters; projects in culture, nature and science.

Award: Typically EUR 15,000 to EUR 50,000 for national project applications by foundations and associations; smaller amounts via provincial chapters and themed funds (EUR 1,500 to EUR 25,000). Individual makers and collectives can request up to EUR 5,000. No application fee.

Duration: Project funding; decision within roughly 2 weeks to 3 months depending on the route.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Het Cultuurfonds (formerly Prins Bernhard Cultuurfonds) invests over EUR 40 million per year in culture and nature in the Netherlands. Foundations and associations can apply year-round for project grants (typically EUR 15,000 to EUR 50,000 at national level), and since 2025 individual makers and collectives can apply for up to EUR 5,000. Provincial chapters run their own quarterly rounds for regional projects, and numerous themed funds (EUR 5,000 to EUR 25,000) cover specific disciplines. Rolling intake via the online application portal. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Fundacion Camila Caneque: International Residency Programme 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Fundacion Camila Caneque with BAR project

Location: Emporda, Catalonia, Spain. Open internationally; individuals only.

Award: EUR 2,000 gross stipend, plus accommodation, meals, utilities, round-trip travel, insurance, and curatorial support. 6 weeks. No application fee.

Duration: Residency 8 October to 20 November 2026.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Fundacion Camila Caneque, with BAR project, hosts a six-week international residency (8 October to 20 November 2026) titled In the meantime nothing happens, in the Emporda region of Catalonia. Open to artists, curators, researchers, and interdisciplinary practitioners; the residency covers a EUR 2,000 gross stipend, accommodation, meals, utilities, round-trip travel, insurance, and curatorial support. Individuals only. Deadline 31 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: AVISTAVULCÃO: Azores Artist Residency 2027 (Faial, Portugal)
item.description:
Organisation: AVISTAVULCÃO / Barca 13

Location: Faial Island, Azores, Portugal (near the Capelinhos Volcano). Open to artists from countries eligible for the Creative Europe programme.

Award: EUR 1,350 allowance per resident for the month, plus travel allowance (EUR 400 to 800), green mobility top-up (EUR 400), visa top-up (EUR 120/resident), outermost regions and overseas territories top-up (EUR 175/resident), family top-up (EUR 200 per child/resident), and accessibility support. No application fee.

Duration: One month, March 2027

Deadline: 01 Aug 2026

AVISTAVULCÃO opens a call for a one-month artist-in-residence programme focused on research and creation, taking place in March 2027 and intended for an artist or artistic duo. Located on the island of Faial near the Capelinhos Volcano (Azores, Portugal), the residency offers immersion in a territory shaped by geology, insularity, and the interplay between nature and human presence, with the Azores treated as living material for creation. Artists are invited to develop projects engaging with landscape, memory, local history, and the contemporary challenges facing peripheral regions; suggested starting points include geological time, transformation, resilience, isolation, mobility, and the relationships between communities and the environment. Context-sensitive proposals that prioritise observation, listening and a relationship with the place are valued. Residents meet local stakeholders and share moments with the community, culminating in a public presentation of the process or results. SUPPORT (in-person mobility): EUR 1,350 allowance per resident, travel allowance of EUR 400 to 800, green mobility top-up of EUR 400, visa top-up of EUR 120 per resident, outermost regions top-up of EUR 175 per resident, family top-up of EUR 200 per child, and accessibility support. ELIGIBILITY: individual artists and duos (cross-disciplinary welcome) from countries eligible for the Creative Europe programme. DEADLINE: 1 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Art Moves Festival: Billboard Art Competition 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Art Moves Festival

Location: Torun, Poland (works shown on city billboards). Open internationally; digital submission.

Award: Main award PLN 6,500 (approx. EUR 1,530); 8 selected works exhibited on billboards across Torun in September 2026. Free entry.

Duration: Exhibition on Torun billboards, September 2026.

Deadline: Closed 15 Jul 2026

The Art Moves Festival in Torun, Poland, runs an annual billboard art competition; the 2026 theme is Fear or courage?. Artists submit digitally, eight selected works are shown on city billboards in September, and the main award is PLN 6,500 (approx. EUR 1,530). Free to enter, open internationally. Deadline 15 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Mondriaan Fund: Residency
item.description:
Organisation: Mondriaan Fund (Mondriaan Fonds)

Location: Netherlands. Applicants must be based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom; residencies may take place in the Netherlands or abroad.

Award: Grant amount varies depending on the location and duration of the residency. Applicants must contribute at least 10% themselves (personal investment, third-party contributions, an interest-free loan, or crowdfunding).

Duration: Varies by residency.

Deadline: 27 Aug 2026

The Mondriaan Fund's Residency grant supports professional visual artists, curators, and art observers who want to deepen their practice through a residency in the Netherlands or abroad. It contributes to the costs of a self-arranged residency that offers meaningful development for the applicant's work. WHO CAN APPLY: visual artists, curators, and art observers who have been professionally active for at least one year and are based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom. AMOUNT: the grant amount varies depending on the residency's location and duration; applicants must contribute at least 10% of the costs themselves, through personal investment, third-party contributions, an interest-free loan, or crowdfunding. APPLYING: submitted through the Mondriaan Fund's online application portal, which requires a pre-created account (allow several working days). DEADLINE: 27 August 2026, 16:00 Dutch time (11:00 Caribbean time).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Arts Council of Ireland: Visual Arts Project Award 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Arts Council of Ireland / An Chomhairle Ealaion

Location: Republic of Ireland. Applicants based outside Ireland may apply if the application clearly shows how outcomes benefit the arts in the Republic of Ireland.

Award: Three strands: up to EUR 12,000 (planning and pre-production), up to EUR 60,000 (production and engagement), up to EUR 150,000 (partnership production). No application fee.

Duration: Projects delivered in 2027.

Deadline: Closed 23 Jul 2026

The Arts Council of Ireland's Visual Arts Project Award funds professional visual artists, curators, and producers to develop and deliver visual arts projects, in three strands: planning and pre-production (up to EUR 12,000), production and engagement (up to EUR 60,000), and partnership production (up to EUR 150,000). Primarily aimed at applicants based in the Republic of Ireland, but the Arts Council's standard eligibility clause allows applicants based elsewhere where the application explicitly demonstrates how the outcomes will benefit the arts in Ireland. The wider Project Award 2027 covers nine disciplines including film and music on a similar timeline. Deadline 23 July 2026, 17:30 Irish time.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Mondriaan Fund: Publication
item.description:
Organisation: Mondriaan Fund (Mondriaan Fonds)

Location: Netherlands. Featured artist(s) must be professional visual artists based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom; publishers may co-apply.

Award: Contribution of up to 35% of eligible expenses. Total budget for this round is EUR 205,000.

Duration: Project-based (production of a publication).

Deadline: 30 Jul 2026

The Mondriaan Fund's Publication grant supports the production of special publications about living visual artists from the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom. It funds books and other publications that give substantial, high-quality attention to an artist's work and practice. WHO CAN APPLY: visual artists and publishers, individually or jointly; the featured artist(s) must be professional and have been active for at least one year, based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom. AMOUNT: the Mondriaan Fund contributes a maximum of 35% of the eligible expenses; the available budget for this round is EUR 205,000. APPLYING: submitted through the Mondriaan Fund's online application portal, which requires a pre-created account (allow several working days to set up). DEADLINE: 30 July 2026, 16:00 Dutch time (10:00 Caribbean time).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Open Call 2027: Galerie mladych (Galerie TIC Brno)
item.description:
Organisation: Galerie TIC Brno

Location: Brno, Czech Republic. Exhibition projects realized at Galerie mladych during 2027.

Award: The gallery covers the financial costs of exhibition installation, an artist/curator fee, collection and transport of the exhibition, and basic PR (photodocumentation included).

Duration: Exhibition project for the year 2027.

Deadline: Closed 20 Jul 2026

Galerie TIC announces an open call for exhibition projects for Galerie mladych (Gallery of Young Artists) for the year 2027. Applicants should send a completed application form, a project description with visual documentation, and an artist's portfolio with CV by midnight on 20 July 2026 via the online form. THE GALLERY OFFERS: to cover the financial costs of exhibition installation; an artist/curator fee; collection and transport of the exhibition; and basic PR (photodocumentation included). APPLICATION: the application form, a short description and visual documentation of the artistic/curatorial project, and a portfolio with CV. Results will be published on the gallery's website and social media no later than the end of August 2026. Deadline 20 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Prisma Art Prize 20th Edition
item.description:
Organisation: Prisma Art Prize

Location: Rome, Italy

Award: EUR 2,000 annual cash fund distributed across quarterly winners, plus group exhibition at Palazzo Merulana, inclusion in the collective catalogue, artist interviews, and eligibility for solo show prizes (Isorrophia Gallery, Pallavicini-Dettori) and partner art residencies (Dar Meso, Casacon, Osservatoriomaree) EUR 34 entry fee for 1 to 3 artworks, plus EUR 5 for each additional artwork up to a maximum of 8

Duration: Group exhibition runs November 5 to 8, 2026

Deadline: 10 Sep 2026

The Prisma Art Prize is an international award for painting and two-dimensional artistic research based in Rome. The 20th edition selects emerging and established artists working in painting, drawing, illustration, printmaking, and traditional mixed media for a group exhibition at Palazzo Merulana, opening November 5, 2026. Winners share an annual cash fund of EUR 2,000 distributed across quarterly calls and receive inclusion in the collective catalogue plus artist interviews. Additional opportunities include solo exhibition prizes and fully funded placements at partner art residencies. The prize is open internationally to all living artists regardless of age, gender, nationality, or educational background. The submission deadline for this edition is September 10, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Artist Residency Autumn 2026 (Centre for Multispecies Relations and Future Ecologies, Palermo)
item.description:
Organisation: Centre for Multispecies Relations and Future Ecologies

Location: Altarello di Baida district, Palermo, Sicily, Italy. On-site 62-day residency, 12 October - 12 December 2026. Open to all nationalities, but applicants must be officially residing in a Creative Europe country (excluding Italy).

Award: Total allowance of EUR 1,860 (before tax) per artist, plus a travel budget of EUR 400 per artist (for distances under 4,000 km) with a EUR 400 green-mobility top-up, plus a production budget. Also provided: on-site accommodation in a fully furnished independent house (private bedroom; shared kitchen, living room, two bathrooms; outdoor studio space 100m from the working site), access to recycled/salvaged materials through the Centre's partnerships, a bicycle per resident, and a final public event.

Duration: 62-day residency, 12 October - 12 December 2026, structured in five phases (orientation, observation/fieldwork, proposal and ecological feasibility, creation, and public restitution).

Deadline: Closed 05 Jul 2026

The Centre for Multispecies Relations and Future Ecologies invites applications from two artists for a 62-day residency (12 October - 12 December 2026) at its site in the Altarello di Baida district of Palermo, Sicily. The residency is structured around a 1,500 sqm garden at the threshold between the city's urban expansion and the historic rural landscape of the surrounding hills; the site, degraded through previous use (soil compaction and significant biodiversity loss), functions as both studio and subject, and artists participate in an ongoing process of ecological transformation, producing interventions that emerge from extended observation and fieldwork. Applications are welcome across all mediums and disciplines (installation, sound, performance, video, food art, participatory practice, and beyond); research-based and interdisciplinary practices are especially encouraged. The Centre works at the intersection of art, science, and (agro)ecological practices, with a focus on multispecies coexistence and climate change in the Mediterranean, and has been based in Palermo since 2021. PROGRAMME: five phases (orientation with small trips and gatherings; observation with independent fieldwork and consultations with artists, curators, scientists, and anthropologists; proposal and ecological feasibility developed with mentors; creation; and public restitution with an open event, a workshop, and a public discussion). Each artist develops an independent site-specific intervention; the two residents share the site, living space, and process, with exchange encouraged but not prescribed. Artist duos and artists who want to collaborate are encouraged to apply (you can apply as an individual for one position, as a duo for both, or as two artists collaborating for two positions). Mentorship by co-founders/artistic directors Fabio Aranzulla and Luca Cinquemani (weekly site visits, studio discussions, work-in-progress presentations); logistics managed by Francesca Farris. WHO: artists whose work engages with ecology, multispecies relations, or the more-than-human, who can move between observation and making and are interested in dialogue with scientists, land workers, and local communities. Priority is given to artists from working-class and less-privileged backgrounds, those who self-identify as part of marginalised communities, and candidates reflecting diversity in gender, age, geographical background, and ability; green travel is prioritised as a condition of participation where feasible. Applicants should be open to living and working in a neighbourhood on the margins of Palermo's periphery. Funded by Culture Moves Europe (Creative Europe programme of the European Union). APPLICATION: first phase via the online form (opened 10 June); shortlisted artists contacted before the end of July; second phase is video calls with shortlisted candidates; applications accepted in English and Italian. Contact: contact.cmrfe@gmail.com. Deadline 5 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: AY-ROOP / Le Milieu: Reuse - (Re)making for Circus Residency 2026 (Rennes Métropole, France)
item.description:
Organisation: AY-ROOP

Location: Le Milieu, Saint-Jacques-de-la-Lande, Rennes Métropole, France. Applicants must legally reside in a Creative Europe country other than France and the French overseas territories (DROM-COM).

Award: Funded through Culture Moves Europe: EUR 900 mobility allowance per person plus flat-rate travel coverage; supplements for green mobility, visa fees, dependent children under 18, OCT/OR residents, and disability/accessibility needs (including possible full coverage of a sign-language interpreter). On-site single-room accommodation, lunches at Le Milieu canteen (Tuesday to Friday), shared kitchen, and technical support provided. No application fee.

Duration: 21-day residency, 28 November to 18 December 2026.

Deadline: Closed 03 Jul 2026

AY-ROOP is launching an open call to select three circus artists and/or circus set designers/builders for a 21-day Culture Moves Europe research residency at Le Milieu (Saint-Jacques-de-la-Lande, Rennes Métropole) from 28 November to 18 December 2026. The residency is a research laboratory on reuse in contemporary circus: how everyday, locally available materials (paper/cardboard, plastics, lightweight metal, textiles, reclaimed wood, bicycle parts, etc.) can be used to create scenographic elements, sets, props and small apparatuses for juggling, acrobatics and balancing. The core principle is that objects are recreated locally from resources available in each territory rather than transported. AY-ROOP provides an equipped construction workshop, a rehearsal stage and circus tent, a guide to local reuse-sector contacts, and a full residency environment (accommodation, shared kitchen, working facilities); two mentors (a reuse specialist and a performing-arts professional) support the artists. PROFILES: circus artists interested in construction/scenography/DIY making, and builders/set designers working in circus; individual or group applications (max 3 people per group); emerging or established, any background. Accessible to artists with disabilities and accessibility needs. Working language English. No aerial apparatus or rigging: all work is ground-based. ELIGIBILITY: aged 18+ (no upper limit); legally resident in a Creative Europe country other than France and DROM-COM; available for the full residency. APPLY with a single PDF (1-page bio/CV, 5-page portfolio or video links, 1-page statement of intent, proof of residence). DEADLINE: 3 July 2026. Results announced 13 July 2026. Check the linked call PDF for full details.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Visual Anthropology Center (VAC): Residency Programme (Belgrade)
item.description:
Organisation: Visual Anthropology Center (VAC)

Location: Belgrade, Serbia (accommodation and studio in central Belgrade; additional workspace at Community Center KROV)

Award: EUR 1,000–1,500 per month (stipend, varies by project requirements), plus accommodation and studio space provided. No application fee.

Duration: Approximately 1–2 months; flexible packages depending on project needs

Deadline: 31 Dec 2027

Visual Anthropology Center (VAC) in Belgrade invites filmmakers, visual anthropologists, artists, and researchers to apply for its residency programme. The residency specialises in visual anthropology but is also open to individual and collaborative projects in experimental ethnography, creative documentary film, and other artistic mediums. Residents may apply with a project aimed at a public showing, or use the time and space to reflect, research, or write without a specific outcome. WHAT IS PROVIDED: accommodation and studio space in central Belgrade; additional workspace and community connection through Community Center KROV (an independent creative hub hosting lectures, events, and exhibitions); project guidance and mentorship from film professionals, ethnomusicologists, producers, artists, curators, and anthropologists; connections to local cultural and academic institutions, archives, galleries, and exhibition spaces; access to camera equipment, translators for fieldwork or editing, fixers, and project-specific services or materials; opportunity to exhibit a final project as an exhibition, installation, screening, workshop, or lecture. Eligible projects may be promoted through VAC's platforms and partner networks, including the Vizantrop Film Festival, for regional and international visibility. ELIGIBILITY: filmmakers, visual anthropologists, artists, and researchers; individual and collaborative projects welcome. Applications accepted on a rolling basis.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stichting Stokroos: Seed Grant 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Stichting Stokroos

Location: Kingdom of the Netherlands (including its Caribbean parts) or Suriname; must have lived and worked there at least one year. Applicants outside the Randstad, or plans taking place there, particularly encouraged.

Award: Fixed grant (no project budget required): EUR 5,000 for an individual, EUR 7,500 for a duo, EUR 10,000 for a collective. Paid in full within 2 weeks of award. No application fee.

Duration: Focused working period; plan starts at least 10 weeks after submission and runs a maximum of one year.

Deadline: 31 Oct 2026

The Stichting Stokroos Seed Grant gives individual makers, duos and collectives time and space to focus on their artistic development, for example a focused working period for research and experiment, or the development of a prototype, new work or methodology. There are two application rounds in 2026: 15 May to 30 June 2026 (now full) and 1 September to 31 October 2026; Stokroos considers a maximum of 60 eligible applications per period (or closes once the budget is reached) and supports around 50 applications per year. Eligibility: active as a visual artist, designer and/or craft practitioner (overlap possible); living and working in the Kingdom of the Netherlands (including its Caribbean parts) or Suriname for at least one year; at least 21 years old; you completed your first subject-specific (vocational or higher) programme between 2018 and 2022, or started without formal training (first presentation marks the start of your practice); not currently enrolled in a study or post-academic programme. Duos and collectives may apply provided all participants meet the guidelines. The grant is a fixed amount with no budget required: EUR 5,000 (individual), EUR 7,500 (duo) or EUR 10,000 (collective). Plans can take place anywhere, start at least 10 weeks after submission and last a maximum of one year. You may apply for the Seed Grant once every two calendar years and receive it up to twice, and may submit only one application per year across all Stokroos grants. Apply via the online form (Dutch or English) with a portfolio PDF up to 20 pages/person, optional video up to 10 minutes total, CV up to 2 pages/person, and work/plan/collaboration descriptions up to 2,500 characters each. Stokroos aims to respond within 8 weeks; no appeal is possible. Next deadline: 31 October 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Simone Veil Prize for European Jewish Cultural Heritage 2026
item.description:
Organisation: European Commission, Directorate-General for Education, Youth, Sport and Culture

Location: EU Member States and third countries associated to the Creative Europe programme

Award: €10,000 per Grand Prix winner (five winners, one per category); up to 25 finalists recognized. No application fee.

Duration: For projects completed between January 2023 and January 2026

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Simone Veil Prize, administered by the European Commission, recognises outstanding projects preserving and promoting European Jewish cultural heritage. It is awarded across five categories: Shared Histories, Heritage for Dialogue, Living Heritage, Cross-border Cooperation and Shared Narratives, and Next Generation/Youth Engagement and Education. AWARD: €10,000 per Grand Prix winner (one per category, five in total); up to 25 finalists receive recognition. ELIGIBILITY: legal or natural persons (individuals or organisations) registered in EU Member States or third countries associated to the Creative Europe programme; projects must have been completed between January 2023 and January 2026. DEADLINE: 31 July 2026 at 23:59 CET. APPLY by submitting the completed application form by email to applications@simoneveilprize.eu.

item.title: Stichting Stokroos: Residency at Fundaziun Nairs 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Stichting Stokroos

Location: Residency in Scuol, Switzerland (Fundaziun Nairs). Applicants must live and work in the Kingdom of the Netherlands (including its Caribbean parts) or Suriname for at least one year. Applicants outside the Randstad particularly encouraged.

Award: Fully supported stay (accommodation and meals paid directly to Fundaziun Nairs) plus an unrestricted living, travel and production budget of EUR 7,000. 80% paid before the residency, the remainder on receipt of a brief final report. No application fee.

Duration: Intensive three-month working period (July through September).

Deadline: 11 Sep 2026

Stichting Stokroos funds a three-month reflective residency at Fundaziun Nairs in Scuol, Switzerland, for one selected visual artist, designer or craft practitioner (overlap possible). Both emerging and established makers may apply. Stokroos covers full accommodation and meals directly with Fundaziun Nairs and provides a discretionary EUR 7,000 budget for living, travel and production. The application should include a proposal that engages specifically with the residency, its Alpine surroundings and/or its historical and cultural context. Eligibility: you completed your first subject-specific (vocational or higher) programme in 2022 or earlier, or have an equivalent established practice, or started without formal training (first presentation marks the start of your practice); at least 21 years old; living and working in the Kingdom of the Netherlands (including its Caribbean parts) or Suriname for at least one year; not currently enrolled in a study or post-academic programme. Applications are individual only; duos and collectives cannot apply. Makers already selected by Fundaziun Nairs may still apply if they meet the criteria. You may submit only one application per year across all Stokroos grants, and must not have received a Seed Grant rejection in the previous calendar year. Apply via the online form (Dutch or English) with a portfolio PDF up to 20 pages, optional video up to 10 minutes total, CV up to 2 pages, and work/plan descriptions up to 2,500 characters each. Selection includes an introductory interview with up to three candidates; Stokroos aims to respond within 8 weeks. No appeal is possible. Application window: 1 to 11 September 2026. More information at nairs.ch.

Open call details

item.title: Stichting Stokroos: Maker Initiatives Grant 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Stichting Stokroos

Location: Kingdom of the Netherlands (including its Caribbean parts) or Suriname; must have lived and worked there at least one year. Applicants outside the Randstad, or plans taking place there, are particularly encouraged.

Award: Fixed grant of EUR 25,000 or EUR 40,000 (chosen depending on need). Maximum participant fee EUR 2,200/month (40-hour week); third-party hire capped at EUR 85/hour excl. VAT. Co-funding not required. No application fee.

Duration: Plan starts in the next calendar year (2027) and runs a maximum of two years.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Stichting Stokroos Maker Initiatives supports established visual artists, designers and craft practitioners (overlap possible), including duos and collectives, in developing and realising a multivocal artistic project or programme centred on a common concern, in collaboration with other makers or communities. There is one application round per year and Stokroos can support 4 to 6 proposals; a maximum of 40 eligible applications are considered per period. Eligibility: you completed your first subject-specific (vocational or higher) programme in 2020 or earlier, or have an equivalent established track record, or started without formal training (first presentation marks the start of your practice); at least 21 years old; living and working in the Kingdom of the Netherlands (including its Caribbean parts) or Suriname for at least one year; not currently enrolled in a study or post-academic programme. You must apply together with an artistic or creative partner (from professional makers to communities); duos and collectives must also have an external partner. You may submit only one application per year across all Stokroos grants, and must not have received a Seed Grant rejection in the previous calendar year. The grant is a fixed contribution of EUR 25,000 or EUR 40,000 depending on need. Plans can take place anywhere, start in the next calendar year and last a maximum of two years. Applications are submitted via the online form in Dutch or English (portfolio PDF up to 20 pages/person, optional video up to 10 minutes total, CV up to 2 pages/person, budget up to 3 pages, work/plan/collaboration descriptions up to 2,500 characters each). Stokroos aims to respond within 10 weeks; no appeal is possible. Application window: 20 to 31 July 2026. Questions: mail@stokroos.nl.

item.title: Amarte Fonds: Development and Project Contributions (rolling)
item.description:
Organisation: Amarte Fonds

Location: Netherlands and the Caribbean part of the Kingdom (the ABC and SSS islands). Applicants must be based there.

Award: Development contribution up to EUR 2,750; project contribution up to EUR 5,500. Up to 80 percent paid in advance, remainder after the report is approved. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based; rolling decisions.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Amarte Fonds is a private Dutch foundation that supports the arts, with rolling (continuous) contributions for visual artists and independent curators. There are two contribution types: a development contribution of up to EUR 2,750 and a project contribution of up to EUR 5,500. Up to 80 percent is paid in advance, with the remainder after the final report is approved. Applicants must be based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom (the ABC and SSS islands). The fund also supports work across theatre, film, music, literature and interdisciplinary projects through specific programmes. There is no fixed deadline and no application fee; decisions are relatively fast. Apply year-round via the online portal.

Open call details

Location: Ljubljana, Slovenia. Open internationally to artists of all nationalities and professional levels.

Duration: Production residency of up to one month.

Deadline: 15 Sep 2026

MoTA (Museum of Transitory Art) in Ljubljana runs an annual open call for production residencies for media, sound and visual artists, curators and researchers. Production residencies offer full support including accommodation, technical support, production costs and an artist fee; the exact artist-fee amount is not published on the official or aggregator pages, so applicants should confirm the figure directly by email. Residencies run up to one month. Open internationally: artists of all nationalities and at all professional levels are encouraged to apply, as individuals or collectives. No application fee. The annual deadline is 15 September (for residencies the following year). Apply by email to mota.artists@gmail.com with the subject line Application for production residency. item.title: FUTURES Photography: 2027 Residency Open Call
item.description:
Organisation: FUTURES Photography

Location: Futures Hub, Amsterdam, Netherlands; ISSP, Riga, Latvia; Capa Center, Budapest, Hungary

Award: EUR 1,000 stipend; accommodation provided. No application fee.

Duration: Minimum 1 month; location-specific dates in 2027 (Amsterdam: Jan, Feb, Apr, May, Jun, Sep, Oct, Nov, Dec; Riga: May; Budapest: Apr)

Deadline: Closed 22 Jun 2026

ELIGIBILITY: Open exclusively to current FUTURES artists (existing members of the FUTURES Photography platform); the application is free of charge. CONCEPT: The residency supports emerging photographers with time, space, and resources to develop new work, with a strong emphasis on research and artistic process. Artists are invited to explore the intersection of Nature, Environment, and Technology - projects should challenge perceptions of human and ecological relationships, technological advancement, and environmental responsibility. An interdisciplinary approach is encouraged, and each residency culminates in a public event where artists share their work and engage with diverse audiences. LOCATIONS: Three partner venues host residencies at different times in 2027. (1) Futures Hub, Amsterdam (global FUTURES HQ): a 60 sqm ground-floor studio with heating, electricity, and private entrance; live-work space with double folding bed, private bathroom, washing machine, and small kitchen; sliding door separates work and living areas; access to a shared atrium for large-scale works, performances, and presentations; communal rooftop terrace; bed linens and towels provided; available January, February, April, May, June, September, October, November, December 2027. (2) ISSP, Riga: private bedroom with working space in a shared city-centre apartment with two other artists; spacious shared living room and fully equipped kitchen; access to ISSP office facilities including printer, scanner, ISSP photography library, and studio lights; available May 2027. (3) Capa Center, Budapest: fully furnished private room in a communal apartment at Art Quarter Budapest; 400 sqm shared studio in a postindustrial area; services to integrate residents into the local Budapest art scene and establish professional connections; option to organise a presentation at Capa Centre in downtown Budapest; access to Capa Centre office facilities (printer, scanner); available April 2027. PRESENCE: minimum 90% presence required throughout the residency period. ENGAGEMENT: residents contribute to a Visual Diary (one Instagram carousel and two Stories, in collaboration with FUTURES), a short Video Portrait documenting their process, a public event such as an open studio or workshop (open studios on the last Wednesday of the month), and a written interview with a local writer published on the FUTURES website. FINANCIAL SUPPORT: EUR 1,000 stipend to cover living expenses, materials, and travel; accommodation is provided at all locations. Residents are responsible for arranging travel documents, health and travel insurance, liability insurance, and any visa requirements (support provided where needed). APPLY: via the FUTURES website; application is free and open only to FUTURES artists. TIMELINE: deadline 22 June 2026 at 23:59 Amsterdam time; results announced July 2026. Contact info@futures-photography.com for questions.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Sandberg Instituut: Call for Proposals, Temporary Department 2027-2029 (Head of Department, The Archive)
item.description:
Organisation: Sandberg Instituut

Location: Amsterdam, Netherlands; applicants must be based in the Netherlands or willing to relocate for the duration of the programme.

Award: Freelance position as Head of the Temporary Department, approx. two days per week over two academic years (September 2027 to July 2029), plus allocated hours for curriculum development (October 2026 to July 2027). Approximate daily rate EUR 553.60. The department is also supported by a budget for core tutors and guest contributors, a dedicated department coordinator plus administrative support, and institutional infrastructure and guidance. A modest fee is provided for the second-round interview stage. No application fee.

Duration: Two-year Temporary Department, September 2027 to July 2029 (curriculum development from October 2026)

Deadline: Closed 05 Jul 2026

Sandberg Instituut invites artists, designers, researchers, makers and educators to propose a Temporary Department, taking on the role of Head of Department for the period September 2027 to July 2029. THEME, THE ARCHIVE: for this edition the institute seeks proposals that engage with the archive as a contemporary artistic, social, political and pedagogical framework. Increasingly recognised as a site of power, selection, memory, erasure and imagination, the archive is approached here not as a static collection but as a living, contested and generative process to be activated, reinterpreted and transformed through contemporary practice, and as a material and methodological field to work with, against and through. Possible starting points include: archives as sites of resistance, repair and restitution; embodied, oral and performative archives; collective and community-based archives; digital, algorithmic and AI-driven processes; counter-archives and practices of refusal; speculative archives and future-oriented forms of memory; creative practices that activate, reinterpret or transform existing archives; and the politics of preservation, access and knowledge production. Proposals should articulate a compelling approach to the archive plus a clear, connected pedagogical vision for how collective research unfolds over two years while supporting individual trajectories; partnerships with external organisations (museums, social archives, artist-led initiatives, community organisations and research institutions) are encouraged. FORMAT: Temporary Departments are full-time two-year master's studies bringing a cohort of students and tutors together around a specific field of inquiry; successful completion leads to a Master in Fine Arts and Design accredited by the NVAO. POSITION: the freelance Head of Department role is equivalent to approx. two days per week over the two academic years plus curriculum-development hours (October 2026 to July 2027), at an approximate daily rate of EUR 553.60, supported by a budget for core tutors and guest contributors, a department coordinator with administrative support, and institutional infrastructure. WHO CAN APPLY: applicants should have teaching/education experience; be able to develop and coordinate a two-year MA curriculum rooted in their own research and practice; have strong communication and leadership skills; have cross-disciplinary collaborative experience; be committed to an inclusive, supportive learning environment responsive to diverse access needs; be based in the Netherlands or willing to relocate; hold an established independent professional practice registered with the Dutch Chamber of Commerce (KVK); and, if applying from outside the EU, hold a valid Dutch residence and work permit for the appointment. SELECTION (two rounds): first-round submissions to td@sandberg.nl by 17:00 on Sunday 5 July 2026 (a max. 500-word department proposal, a max. 200-word networks and collaborations statement, and a max. 2-page CV); first-round notification 15 July 2026; interviews with shortlisted candidates 16 September 2026. Questions: td@sandberg.nl.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.description:

Location: Weimar, Germany. Open to international artists; winning works are projected onto historic facades during the festival.

Award: EUR 7,000 production budget per winning facade (one winner selected per building) plus a EUR 3,000 festival prize for one selected work. No application fee.

Duration: Festival 11-13 September 2026; deliverable is a roughly 5-minute projection-mapping work for a facade.

Deadline: Closed 14 Jun 2026

Genius Loci Weimar is an annual projection-mapping (video mapping) festival that invites artists to design audiovisual works for the facades of historic Weimar buildings. The 2026 competition theme is Democracy and Society. Entry is at concept stage: submissions are conceptual proposals, and professional video-mapping expertise is not required, as the organisers provide technical support to realise winning concepts. One winner is selected per facade and receives a EUR 7,000 production budget; one selected work additionally receives a EUR 3,000 festival prize. Open to international artists working as individuals or in teams. Winners are announced around 6 July 2026 and the festival runs 11-13 September 2026. Application deadline: 14 June 2026, 23:59 CET. Register and submit via the official competition page.

item.title: Treehouse NDSM: Open Call, Come Back to Your ___ (Nature/Culture) Exhibition 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Treehouse NDSM

Location: Treehouse NDSM Pavilion, NDSM, Amsterdam-Noord, Netherlands; particular interest in artists from Amsterdam Noord.

Award: An artist fee is provided to all participating artists. Selected works are exhibited on the 100 m2 mezzanine of Treehouse NDSM's Pavilion. No application fee. (Artist fee amount not stated.)

Duration: Exhibition 17 September to 11 October 2026

Deadline: Closed 28 Jun 2026

Treehouse NDSM invites artists to apply for its next exhibition, 'Come Back to Your ___ (Nature/Culture)', running 17 September to 11 October 2026. In the context of current ecological crises, the exhibition takes the constant interaction between culture and nature as its starting point, noting how societies shape the land (from Japanese gardens to post-Soviet geometric parks, Dutch dikes and polders, and Indigenous Australian cultural burning) and how environments shape societies in turn. Artists are invited to reflect on questions such as: how do nature and culture share the same ground? How has your community shaped the land, and how has the land shaped your community? The exhibition is part of Art Park, a series launched at Treehouse in 2023 that reflects on urban nature, particularly in Amsterdam-Noord. The organisers welcome projects that do not offer answers but dig into questions and leave room for uncertainty, across media including performance, painting, making, installation and video. SELECTION: the committee (artist Alina Bielun and artistic coordinator Mulan Go) will select up to ten artworks for the 100 m2 mezzanine of Treehouse NDSM's Pavilion, considering both relevance to the theme and the chosen medium, with particular interest in the voices of artists from Amsterdam Noord (temporary or performative works that meaningfully complement the exhibition are also welcome). An artist fee is provided to all participants. Treehouse NDSM is not a traditional gallery but a multidisciplinary incubator offering 100 affordable studios, exhibition spaces and a collaborative artistic community. Submission deadline: 28 June 2026. Apply via the online form.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CreArt 3.0: Residency at Chalet Lecoq 2026 (Clermont-Ferrand)
item.description:
Organisation: City of Clermont-Ferrand (CreArt network)

Location: Chalet Lecoq, Jardin Lecoq, Clermont-Ferrand, France.

Award: Residency grant EUR 3,000 per artist for the two-month residency (EUR 1,500/month), plus EUR 600 production costs per artist. For European (CreArt network) artists: reimbursement of round-trip travel up to EUR 500, and accommodation (a two-bedroom apartment) plus a fully-equipped workspace; the Clermont-Ferrand artist receives the workspace. No application fee.

Duration: Two-month residency, 26 October to 20 December 2026

Deadline: Closed 21 Jun 2026

As part of the CreArt network, the City of Clermont-Ferrand in France is organising a two-month residency for three visual artists at Chalet Lecoq, selecting two artists from the CreArt network and one from Clermont-Ferrand. Both central and atypical, set in the middle of a public garden (the Jardin Lecoq, a park in the heart of the city), the Chalet Lecoq is the former janitor's house of the park and invites reflection on the relationship between nature and the urban, in a place frequented by residents of all ages. It offers a privileged setting with a two-bedroom apartment and a fully-equipped workspace, and welcomes international artists in residency throughout the year. An end-of-residency presentation may be organised in discussion with the hosted artists. CONDITIONS: residency grant of EUR 3,000 per artist (EUR 1,500/month); EUR 600 production costs per artist; for European artists, reimbursement of round-trip travel up to EUR 500; accommodation and workspace for the two European artists, and workspace for the Clermont-Ferrand artist. ELIGIBILITY: artists born or resident in one of the following CreArt cities (Kaunas, Lithuania; Liepaja, Latvia; Skopje, North Macedonia; Aveiro, Portugal; Valladolid, Spain; Lublin, Poland; Venice, Italy; Rouen, France; Ceske Budejovice, Czech Republic; Oulu, Finland; Regensburg, Germany); members of the Croatian Association of Visual Artists (HDLU); and Ukrainian artists through cooperation with the Lviv Art Council 'Dialog'. APPLY in English (a single PDF with ID/passport and residency certificate if required, a one-page CV, a portfolio of max 10 pages/10 images, and a one-page project proposal) via the CreArt artists' platform. A jury of Clermont-Ferrand contemporary art professionals selects the artists, with particular attention to work that may reveal forms of connection. Deadline: 21 June 2026 (midnight); selected candidates announced 3 July 2026.

Open call details

item.title: SVĚTOVA 1: As We Grow V (Educational & Research Program) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: SVĚTOVA 1

Location: Prague, Czech Republic; participants must be physically present in Prague for the entire program.

Award: Scholarship of CZK 5,000 (around EUR 200). Free 14-day educational programme; work exhibited at SVĚTOVA 1 for at least six weeks with production and PR support. NO accommodation provided (funding restrictions). No application fee.

SVĚTOVA 1 invites early-career artists and cultural organizers to apply for the fifth edition of its educational and research programme As We Grow. This year the course is led by artist and mythopoet András Cséfalvay, Associate Professor at the Academy of Fine Arts in Bratislava. The 14-day project (7 to 20 September 2026) culminates in a group exhibition. WHAT YOU GET: a free 14-day educational experience; a scholarship of CZK 5,000 (around EUR 200); a rich working environment with on-site interaction with the leading artist, scholars and other artists; active involvement in the As We Grow research and event programming; organizational and professional support; and exhibition of your work at SVĚTOVA 1 for at least six weeks, with production and PR support before, during and after the show. SVĚTOVA 1 actively encourages queer and BIPoC people who have migrated to or taken refuge in Prague to apply. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be physically present in Prague for the entire program, have essential spoken English, and commit to the whole 14 days after acceptance. Note: accommodation cannot be offered due to funding restrictions. APPLY via the online Notion form with a portfolio of 3 works, a CV (max 2 pages), and either a 2-3 minute video or a 250-word motivation letter (may include images) outlining how your lived experience and interest align with the course. Deadline: 30 June 2026 at midnight; results announced by 15 July 2026. Questions: tomas@svetova1.cz.

Open call details

item.title: TRACE: Artistic Residencies on Memory, Displacement & Contemporary Europe 2026-27
item.description:
Organisation: Camera a Sud (Italy), Forgotten Children of War Association (Bosnia and Herzegovina) and The 4th Block (Ukraine)

Location: Italy, Bosnia and Herzegovina, and online (Ukraine); open to artists based in Creative Europe programme countries.

Award: NO daily artist fee. Travel, accommodation and meals covered during programme activities (up to the maximum allowed under Creative Europe regulations). Technical equipment for producing artworks is NOT provided. Plus international visibility/PR, exhibitions, mentorship and a certificate. No application fee.

Duration: Research missions in 2026 (Bosnia and Herzegovina Jul/Aug, Italy Oct, Ukraine online); residencies in 2027 for 6 selected artists

Deadline: Closed 18 Jun 2026

TRACE is an international artistic residency programme, funded by the EU's Creative Europe programme, exploring how contemporary art can engage with memory, war, displacement, ecological crisis and the collective experiences shaping Europe today. It takes as starting points recent and ongoing crises, including the conflict in Bosnia and Herzegovina and the Srebrenica genocide, Mediterranean migration, the ecological legacy of Chernobyl, and the Russian invasion of Ukraine, bringing together artists, curators, researchers and local communities to work with archives, sites of memory and present-day social realities. WHO THEY SEEK: artists whose practice engages themes such as memory and remembrance, identity, war and its consequences, migration and displacement, archives and documentary practices, the relationship between past and present, and ecological and social transformation, working across visual arts, photography, film and video, sound art, illustration, graphic storytelling, podcasts and audio, or interdisciplinary practice. No age restrictions. ELIGIBILITY: artists based in countries participating in Creative Europe (EU member states; Iceland, Norway, Liechtenstein; and accession/candidate/potential-candidate countries including Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Georgia, Kosovo, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia, Ukraine, Armenia and Tunisia). STRUCTURE: an international jury selects 15 participants for three research and workshop programmes (Bosnia and Herzegovina Jul/Aug 2026, Italy Oct 2026, Ukraine online); 6 artists are then selected to continue into the 2027 residency programme (on-site in Italy and Bosnia and Herzegovina, plus an online residency connected to Ukraine with The 4th Block), with outcomes shown in international exhibitions in the partner countries (the Ukrainian exhibition in a safe location in Lviv) and the TRACE digital archive. WHAT PARTICIPANTS RECEIVE: coverage of travel, accommodation and meals during programme activities (up to Creative Europe limits), international visibility and PR support, access to archives and communities, mentorship, professional documentation and a certificate; there is NO daily artist fee and no production equipment. English is the working language. APPLY via the Google Form with a portfolio (max 5MB PDF) or website, a short project proposal/research idea (max 1 page) and a short biography. Deadline: 18 June 2026 (Rome time, UTC+2); selected participants contacted by 28 June. Questions: info@tracearts.eu.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Kasteel Wijlre estate: The Resonant Garden Residency 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Kasteel Wijlre estate

Location: Kasteel Wijlre estate, Wijlre, South Limburg, the Netherlands; open to artists living in the EU.

Award: Stipend of EUR 800 per month for three months (EUR 2,400 total) plus production and research compensation up to EUR 2,000. Private accommodation and a small in-house studio provided. All other costs (insurance, food, transport) covered by the resident. No application fee.

Duration: 3 months, September to November 2026 (exact dates to be discussed)

Deadline: Closed 28 Jun 2026

Kasteel Wijlre estate invites international artists to apply for The Resonant Garden, a three-month residency set within the gardens and grounds of the estate in the hills of South Limburg. The garden is considered a place of cultivation and care, memory and imagination, rooted histories and possible futures; artists are invited to engage with it as a site for artistic research, hands-in-the-ground material experimentation and poetic speculation. Residents have access to the gardens and a small (16 sqm) in-house studio, with basic gardening and technical tools provided; the site itself, its green exterior, its historical views on 'the garden' and its embeddedness in the South Limburg landscape, becomes both companion and collaborator. Feedback sessions with garden and art professionals from the estate's network run throughout, concluding with an open studio event or presentation, and the residency sits within the Borderlands Residency network (networking and field trips). FOR WHOM: artists living in the EU with both research- and studio-based practices who wish to engage with the garden as a metaphor, method and/or material environment, and who embrace the rural location and the quiet and relative isolation of a country estate. ACCOMMODATION: private living and studio space (kitchen, bathroom, bedroom) in the castle courtyard; a bicycle is provided and the estate is reachable by public transport (Heerlen 12 km, Aachen 17 km, Maastricht 19 km). PRESENCE: the resident is expected to spend most of the period on site and to take part in at least two (semi-)public moments (an introductory presentation and a concluding open studio), and to be available for exchanges with the estate's professional network. BUDGET: EUR 800/month for three months plus production and research compensation up to EUR 2,000; all other costs (insurance, food, transport) are the resident's. SELECTION: a panel of two artist-advisors and the director-curator review applications on artistic vision, strength of proposal, and resonance with the estate context. APPLY: send a single PDF with a motivation statement and project proposal (max 500 words) and a CV (max 2 pages) to residency coordinator Anne Vangronsveld, a.vangronsveld@kasteelwijlre.nl. Deadline: 28 June 2026; decisions communicated the week of 6 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Platform: Residency Program 2027 (Loosey-Goosey), Vaasa, Finland
item.description:
Organisation: Platform

Location: Vaasa, Finland; artist must spend at least 80 percent of the residency in Vaasa.

Award: Fully funded. Working grant of EUR 42/day (proportional to days spent in Vaasa), a material budget up to EUR 1,000 (reimbursed against original receipts for project-related purchases), and travel covered (one return trip, cheapest option). Accommodation provided. No application fee.

Duration: 4 to 12 weeks during 2027 (applicant specifies preferred months)

Deadline: Closed 30 Jun 2026

Platform is an art organization in Vaasa, a relatively peripheral Finnish town, running an art space and a residency. The 2027 theme, Loosey-Goosey, invites working artists to explore the limits of their expression and question professionalism: what happens to our art when we define and limit it to conform to society's lines, and who decides what makes a professional artist. Platform seeks artists who work with the topic of professionalism, questioning it or rejecting result-driven societal pressures and norms. The residency is open to artists of any medium, including but not limited to installation, performance, sound art, textiles, sculpture, video, community art, and painting. SUPPORT: a fully funded residency with a private apartment (bedroom and kitchen) within walking distance of the dedicated studio and project space; a working grant of EUR 42/day (proportional to days spent in Vaasa); a material budget up to EUR 1,000 reimbursed against receipts; and one return trip (cheapest option) covered. A contact person helps the artist settle in, source materials and make connections. Platform holds basic tools and electronics (projectors, Zoom recorder, office printer, sound system, sewing machine, etc.). DURATION: 4 to 12 weeks during 2027; artists who can stay up to 12 weeks are preferred, though 4-8 week requests are considered, and duos may apply. EXPECTATIONS: give a public talk, performance, workshop or presentation at the start; communicate in English, Swedish or Finnish; spend at least 80 percent of the residency in Vaasa; and submit project documentation plus a short report for the website. Platform does not run a gallery, so no end-of-residency exhibition is offered, but it will help arrange a final presentation, studio visit or performance if wanted. APPLY: via the online form only, with a single PDF (max 10 pages, 5 MB) named FIRSTNAME_LASTNAME containing (1) an informal one-page project plan and motivation letter stating preferred 2027 period, (2) descriptions and images of 5 previous projects or artworks (direct video links for videos), and (3) a CV of max two pages including name, date of birth and current location. Open call: 8 June to 30 June 2026, 23:59 EEST. All applicants notified by the end of September 2026; the board makes the selection and is not obliged to justify it.

Open call details

item.title: ARCAthens: Athens Residency Fellowships (Fall 2026)
item.description:
Organisation: ARCAthens

Location: Athens, Greece (in-person residency). Open to applicants not residing in Greece

Award: Fully funded: USD 3,000 Fellowship Prize, USD 200/week stipend toward food and local transport, basic coach round-trip airfare reimbursed, private bedroom and bathroom with shared kitchen, and studio access for Visual Art Fellows. No application fee.

Duration: 3 months, 10 September to 30 November 2026

Deadline: Closed 30 Jun 2026

The Athens Residency is the flagship program of ARCAthens, a not-for-profit founded in 2017, offering two fully-funded Fellowships (Visual Art and Curatorial) that enable artists, curators, and scholars to live and work in Athens, Greece, and respond to the city's ongoing cultural renaissance. The residency is a structured, research-driven program built around scheduled engagements with artists, curators, institutions, and collections, including arranged studio and private collection visits, guided institutional access, and select community events. Distinct from studio-isolated models, it emphasizes engagement and exchange, balancing focused visits with time for independent exploration. ELIGIBILITY: ARCAthens welcomes applicants from all countries and nationalities representing a broad range of creative thought and practice; the program is open to applicants not residing in Greece. PRACTICAL: 3-month duration; USD 3,000 Fellowship Prize; USD 200 per week stipend toward food and local transportation; a basic coach round-trip ticket is provided (Fellows purchase following ARCAthens approval of itinerary and cost, reimbursed within five business days; in cases of financial hardship ARCAthens may arrange travel directly). Fellows are responsible for all other expenses, including food, local transportation, and any production, installation, or shipping costs. Visas are not necessary for US nationals for the duration of the program; others are responsible for procuring visas as applicable. Facilities include a private bedroom and bathroom with shared kitchen; Visual Art Fellows have studio access. Two Fellows are in residence at one time. Working language is English. Selection is by a 3-person independent committee of arts professionals. No fee, open call. TIMELINE: application deadline 30 June 2026 (closes 11:00 p.m. ET); notification of Fellowships mid July; residency 10 September to 30 November 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CIRCUS LOOPS: European Residency Open Call 2026-2028
item.description:
Organisation: Circusstad Festival (NL), Zirkus ON (DE) and Cirkus Kolektiv (HR), co-funded by Creative Europe

Location: Residencies in Split (Croatia), Karlsruhe (Germany) and Rotterdam (Netherlands)

Award: Each selected core team member receives a fixed grant of EUR 3,746; travel, accommodation and per diems are covered by the project partners. No application fee.

Duration: Three 10-day residencies between November 2026 and July 2027; final presentations at partner festivals 2027-2028

Deadline: Closed 17 Jun 2026

CIRCUS LOOPS is a European cooperation project (2026-2028) led by Circusstad Festival (NL), Zirkus ON (DE) and Cirkus Kolektiv (HR), co-funded by the Creative Europe Programme, inviting contemporary circus artists and collectives to a residency programme dedicated to developing new work through audience engagement. It centres on the Feedback Loops methodology, integrating audience perspectives into the artistic creation process. Selected projects take part in three 10-day international residencies in Split, Karlsruhe and Rotterdam between November 2026 and July 2027 (all projects in residence simultaneously in each location, working in separate spaces), presenting work-in-progress and engaging in facilitated feedback sessions with audiences, peers and experts. The programme concludes with final presentations at partner festivals between 2027 and 2028. ELIGIBILITY: contemporary circus artists and small companies based in Europe (including Creative Europe countries) developing a project at an early or mid stage of creation; applicants must be available for all residency periods and commit to the full programme; teams limited to three core members. SUPPORT: a fixed grant of EUR 3,746 per core team member, plus travel, accommodation and per diems covered. APPLY via the official online form (project description, artistic team info, motivation and video material), in English, by 17 June 2026, 23:59 CET. An international jury selects three projects on artistic quality, motivation, feasibility and relevance to the Feedback Loops methodology; results published by 25 July 2026. Contact: circusloops@gmail.com.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Museum Dhondt-Dhaenens: MDD Collective Open Call 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Museum Dhondt-Dhaenens (MDD) youth collective

Location: Museum Dhondt-Dhaenens garden, Sint-Martens-Latem, Belgium (outdoor presentation)

Award: EUR 500 material budget plus a EUR 350 participation fee per selected artist; transport and insurance of the works are fully covered by the organisation. No application fee.

Duration: One-weekend presentation (11-12 July 2026)

Deadline: Closed 14 Jun 2026

Open call from Museum Dhondt-Dhaenens' youth collective inviting three to four emerging artists to present a work in the museum garden over the weekend of 11-12 July 2026, in dialogue with its surroundings, architecture and landscape. The theme centres on the idea of the 'frame', starting from Isa Genzken's work Fenster in the museum garden, exploring how a frame determines what we see and what stays just outside our field of vision. ELIGIBILITY: emerging artists aged 31 or under interested in joining MDD's youth project. Apply via the Museum Dhondt-Dhaenens open-call page; questions to opencall@museumdd.be.

item.title: Jan van Eyck Academie: Material Research Fellowships 2027 (Future Materials)
item.description:
Organisation: Jan van Eyck Academie (Future Materials / Future Threads programme, co-funded by the EU Just Transition Fund)

Location: Maastricht, Netherlands (residency; fellows must reside within 10km)

Award: Monthly stipend of EUR 1,750 (80% exempt from income tax) plus an annual working budget of EUR 2,250, a private studio apartment, and access to the Jan van Eyck Labs and external partners. Application fee EUR 50 excl. VAT (waived for applicants from DAC-list lower-income countries and territories).

Duration: 11 months (15 January to 15 December 2027)

Deadline: Closed 24 Jun 2026

Two material-research fellowships at the Jan van Eyck Academie under the Future Materials programme: one Fashion & Textile Material Fellowship and one Future Materials Fellowship, each lasting 11 months. The programme supports environmentally conscious art and design practices researching sustainable, non-toxic, biobased alternatives to fossil-based or toxic materials. ELIGIBILITY: applicants with an existing research project on sustainable/biobased materials (the Fashion & Textile stream focuses on fashion/textile systems; the Future Materials stream on biobased materials or processes for broad creative use). Projects should take a systemic perspective on the just transition. Jan van Eyck alumni, and full-time alumni of the Rijksakademie or De Ateliers, are not eligible. Fellows develop their project with the Jan van Eyck Labs and Maastricht partners (Textile Innovation Maastricht; CHILL at Brightlands Chemelot Campus). Apply via the respective Jan van Eyck application form.

Open call details

item.title: ifa: Rave Scholarship - Cultural Heritage and Museum Transformation (2027)
item.description:
Organisation: ifa (Institut fuer Auslandsbeziehungen) - Alexander Rave Foundation

Location: Host institutions in Germany (applicants must be cultural practitioners from countries on the OECD DAC list)

Award: Net payment of EUR 1,500 per month; monthly family allowances for accompanying family (EUR 250 spouse, EUR 250 per child); visa costs; outward and return travel costs; health insurance (including family); monthly public transport pass for the city of residence; up to EUR 500 for a language course. No application fee.

Duration: 3 to 6 months at a German host institution (with optional supplementary 2-12 week tandem stay in the DAC-list country if synergies arise; both stays in the same year)

Deadline: Closed 30 Jun 2026

Scholarship from ifa (Institut fuer Auslandsbeziehungen), supported by the Alexander Rave Foundation, on the management of cultural heritage and the transformation of museums and exhibition centres. It enables cultural practitioners from countries on the OECD DAC list to stay with host institutions in order to deepen their professional knowledge transnationally, introduce new perspectives and build long-term cooperation through exchange. The focus is on transcultural dialogue and engaging with cultural heritage, collections and archive records, funding mutual knowledge transfer and the creation of sustainable networks, and supporting freedom in art and science, freedom of opinion and open debates. Thematic focal areas centre on transforming museums and exhibition venues in line with the ICOM definition of museums (2023): cultural participation, sustainability, non-discriminatory spaces and decolonial working methods. ELIGIBILITY: curators, restorers, mediators and culture managers from DAC-list countries. Scholarships begin after 1 February 2027.

Open call details
